channel.title: CyberNetSec - Latest Cybersecurity Articles
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity threat intelligence articles from the past 3 days

item.title: LogiTrans Global Paralyzed by $45M QuantumLock Ransomware Attack, Global Supply Chains Disrupted
item.description:
Global logistics firm LogiTrans Global has been hit by a devastating ransomware attack attributed to a new group named QuantumLock. The attack, which began on June 30, 2026, has encrypted critical systems, causing a worldwide halt in operations, including shipment tracking and fleet management. The ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Actively Exploited 'ShiftScribe' Zero-Day in Apex-Office Suite Puts Millions at Risk
item.description:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability, named 'ShiftScribe' and tracked as CVE-2026-35801, has been discovered in the popular Apex-Office Suite, affecting over 100 million users. The flaw, with a CVSS score of 9.6, is being actively exploited by the state-sponsored threat acto...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: HealthNet Insurance Breach Exposes Sensitive Health Data of 11 Million Patients via Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket
item.description:
HealthNet Insurance, a major US health provider, has disclosed a data breach that exposed the personal and medical information of 11 million individuals. The breach, discovered on June 15, 2026, originated from a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket that contained access keys to other s...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cloud Security, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'SandViper' APT Hits Global Energy Sector with New 'DuneStalker' Espionage Malware
item.description:
A new report from Dragos Inc. details a sophisticated cyber-espionage campaign targeting the global energy sector by a previously unknown threat actor named 'SandViper'. The campaign, active since late 2025, focuses on oil and gas companies in the Middle East and Europe. SandViper uses a novel modul...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Malware, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: CodeStream CI/CD Utility Compromised in Supply Chain Attack, Developer Secrets Stolen
item.description:
The popular open-source CI/CD utility 'CodeStream' has been the target of a sophisticated supply chain attack. An unknown threat actor compromised the project's package registry, publishing a malicious version (3.4.1) on June 29, 2026. The tainted version, downloaded over 50,000 times, was designed ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Microsoft Releases Emergency Patch for Actively Exploited Exchange Server RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-17747)
item.description:
Microsoft has released an emergency, out-of-band security update for a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server 2016 and 2019. Tracked as CVE-2026-17747 with a CVSS score of 9.8, the flaw allows an unauthenticated attacker to gain code execution by sending a sp...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'FinReact' Phishing Campaign Uses AI-Generated Lures and 'GhostScript' Loader to Target Banks
item.description:
A highly sophisticated phishing campaign named 'FinReact' is targeting the financial services industry. Researchers at Proofpoint report that the campaign uses generative AI to create convincing, context-aware email lures that bypass traditional security. The attack chain involves a password-protect...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: DataHaven Cloud Storage Outage Caused by Security Breach Targeting Customer Data
item.description:
Enterprise cloud storage provider DataHaven has confirmed that a multi-hour global outage on June 30, 2026, was the result of a security breach. Attackers gained access to an internal Kubernetes cluster via a zero-day vulnerability in a proprietary management API. They then attempted to issue comman...

Severity: High

Categories: Cloud Security, Cyberattack, Incident Response

Read Full Article →

item.title: EU Unveils 'CYBER-AI Act' with Strict Security Mandates and Heavy Fines for High-Risk AI
item.description:
The European Commission has introduced the 'Cybersecurity of Artificial Intelligence Act' (CYBER-AI Act), a new regulation that imposes stringent cybersecurity requirements on high-risk AI systems. Working alongside the existing AI Act, this legislation mandates secure-by-design principles, threat m...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'Synapse' Wiper Malware Disguised as Ransomware, Designed for Maximum Destruction
item.description:
Kaspersky researchers have published a detailed analysis of 'Synapse,' a new and highly destructive malware strain. Initially mistaken for ransomware, Synapse is a pure wiper designed to cause irreversible data loss. It uses deception by dropping a fake ransom note and encrypting a few non-essential...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Malware, Threat Actor, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: One Year Later: Omni Hotels Details $50M Recovery and Security Overhaul After Devastating Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Omni Hotels & Resorts has published a detailed report outlining its recovery and security transformation one year after a catastrophic ransomware attack in 2025. The incident, attributed to the BlackSuit gang, cost the company over $50 million and led to a complete overhaul of its cybersecurity infr...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Incident Response, Security Operations, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI-Powered Disinformation is Top Threat to 2028 Elections, Mandiant Warns
item.description:
A new report from Mandiant (part of Google Cloud) identifies AI-powered disinformation and influence operations as the most significant cyber threat to the 2028 U.S. election cycle. The report warns of a future filled with hyper-realistic deepfakes and AI-driven social media manipulation designed to...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Phantom Squatting: Attackers Weaponize AI-Hallucinated Domains in Novel Supply Chain Attacks
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers from Unit 42 have identified a new and active software supply chain attack vector dubbed 'Phantom Squatting.' The threat involves adversaries exploiting the tendency of Large Language Models (LLMs) to 'hallucinate,' or invent, non-existent web domains for legitimate brands....

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: SimpleHelp RMM Flaw Actively Exploited to Deploy Novel Djinn Stealer
item.description:
A critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-48558, in SimpleHelp remote support software is being actively exploited to gain administrative access to servers. Attackers are leveraging this 10.0 CVSS flaw to deploy a new, cross-platform information stealer called Djinn Stealer via a load...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Malware, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Microsoft Dismantles "StegoAd," a Malicious Edge Extension Campaign Using Steganography
item.description:
Microsoft has disrupted a major malicious browser extension campaign named 'StegoAd' that affected its Edge Add-ons store for over two years. The operation involved 119 extensions, downloaded by up to 2.6 million users, which used steganography to hide malicious JavaScript payloads within image and ...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Supply Chain Attack, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: "GuardFall" Flaw Lets Old Bash Tricks Bypass Modern AI Agent Security
item.description:
A new class of vulnerability named 'GuardFall' has been discovered, affecting ten out of eleven popular open-source AI coding agents. The flaw allows attackers to bypass the agents' security guards using decades-old Bash shell obfuscation tricks. By crafting malicious text in a file like a README, a...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical Pre-Auth RCE Flaw in libssh2 Library Poses Widespread Risk
item.description:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, CVE-2026-55200, has been disclosed in libssh2, a widely used open-source SSH client library. The flaw, rated 9.8 on the CVSS scale, is a pre-authentication heap buffer overflow that can be triggered by a malicious SSH server. Because libssh2 is e...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: EDPB Adopts Common Template for GDPR Data Breach Notifications
item.description:
The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) has adopted a common, standardized template for organizations to use when notifying supervisory authorities of a personal data breach under the GDPR. This initiative aims to simplify the compliance process and harmonize the currently fragmented reporting req...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA
item.description:
Security researchers are warning about increasingly sophisticated email attacks, including the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. This adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attack uses a reverse proxy to present the victim with a real Microsoft login page. When the user authenticates and co...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: TUANZ Calls for "Security by Design" in New Zealand's Digital Future
item.description:
The Tech Users Association of New Zealand (TUANZ) has published a new position paper urging a fundamental shift in the nation's cybersecurity strategy. The group is calling for a move away from the current 'user beware' model, which places a heavy burden on individuals and SMEs. Instead, TUANZ advoc...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Wipro and Palo Alto Networks Launch AI-Driven Managed Detection and Response Service
item.description:
Wipro and Palo Alto Networks have expanded their partnership to launch a new AI-driven Managed Detection and Response (MDR) service. The collaboration combines Palo Alto Networks' Cortex XSIAM platform with Wipro's CyberShield services to provide more proactive and automated threat defense for enter...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →

item.title: AssuranceAmerica Data Breach Impacts Over 1.1 Million, Exposing SSNs and Driver's Licenses
item.description:
AssuranceAmerica Managing General Agency, LLC has disclosed a major data breach affecting over 1.1 million individuals. The incident, which occurred in March 2026, stemmed from a targeted attack on an employee. The unauthorized access led to the theft of a vast amount of sensitive policyholder data,...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Malware and Evasion Techniques in Ukraine War
item.description:
The Russian APT group Gamaredon has continued to evolve its malware arsenal and evasion tactics in its relentless cyber campaign against Ukraine. Recent analysis shows the group is increasingly using legitimate cloud services like Cloudflare for C2, collaborating with other Russian APTs like Turla, ...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Accenture and ServiceNow Unveil Joint AI Offering to Modernize Enterprise Risk Management
item.description:
Accenture and ServiceNow have launched a joint offering that combines AI-powered managed security services with an automated migration solution. The platform aims to help organizations move from legacy risk systems to a modern, unified platform, enabling them to combat the rising speed and sophistic...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Polymarket Hit by $3.1M Supply Chain Attack; Malicious JavaScript Siphons User Funds
item.description:
The cryptocurrency prediction platform Polymarket has confirmed a loss of approximately $3.1 million due to a frontend supply chain attack. First reported on June 26, 2026, the incident occurred when attackers compromised an unnamed third-party software dependency and injected malicious JavaScript i...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Cyberattack, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Over 200,000 Scam Sites Powered by Legitimate Chinese DCloud Framework, Infoblox Reports
item.description:
Cybersecurity firm Infoblox has uncovered a massive network of over 200,000 fraudulent websites being powered by a legitimate Chinese open-source framework called DCloud Uni-App. Threat actors are creating and selling scam templates built with the toolkit, enabling a wide array of investment scams, ...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Fortinet Launches FortiSOC, an AI-Powered Unified Security Operations Platform
item.description:
Fortinet has launched FortiSOC, a new, cloud-delivered Security Operations Center (SOC) platform designed to unify six key security functions into a single SaaS offering. The platform leverages 'agentic AI' to help security teams automate investigations, correlate alerts across assets and identities...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Redwood AI Enters Post-Quantum Cryptography Market with Acquisition of Quantum.IQ
item.description:
Redwood AI Corp. has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Quantum.IQ Technologies, a Vancouver-based company specializing in AI-driven, post-quantum cryptography (PQC) solutions. The move marks Redwood AI's strategic expansion from its focus on artificial intelligence into the emerging and...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence, Other

Read Full Article →
